req.title: Hr-Admin updates timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a timesheet through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
preconditions: authenticated
role:hr-admin
timesheet module is enabled

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers authenticated hr-admin updating a timesheet via REST API with mandatory field validation and audit record creation. I selected five tests: (1) EP positive — valid payload with hr-admin credentials returns 200/201 and audit record is written; (2) NEG — missing mandatory field returns 400; (3) NEG — unauthenticated request returns 401; (4) NEG — authenticated user with wrong role (employee) returns 403; (5) BVA — payload where a numeric hours field is exactly at its minimum boundary (0) to probe edge acceptance. State Transition Coverage is embedded in the positive test by verifying the timesheet moves from its pre-update state to updated state. Decision Table logic covers the auth/role/payload combination matrix across the negative tests.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_timesheet_valid_payload
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is available
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet with a known ID exists in a non-final state
actions: PUT /api/timesheets/{timesheet_id} with all mandatory fields and valid hr-admin token
Query audit log for the timesheet_id
expected: Response body contains the updated timesheet entity ID
Timesheet record reflects the submitted changes
An audit record for the update action exists
trace: R-WEB-018: hr-admin can update timesheet via REST API
R-WEB-018: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-018: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_hr_admin_update_timesheet_valid_payload(
    hr_admin_token,        # <fixture>: returns Bearer token for hr-admin
    existing_timesheet_id, # <fixture>: returns id of a seeded timesheet
    audit_log_client,      # <fixture>: client to query audit records
):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "emp-001",
        "period_start": "2024-06-01",
        "period_end": "2024-06-07",
        "total_hours": 40,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/timesheets/{existing_timesheet_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id") == existing_timesheet_id
    assert body.get("total_hours") == 40
    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=existing_timesheet_id, action="update")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "No audit record found for update"

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is available
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet with a known ID exists
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'period_start'
trace: R-WEB-018: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_hr_admin_update_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(
    hr_admin_token,
    existing_timesheet_id,
):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "emp-001",
        # 'period_start' intentionally omitted
        "period_end": "2024-06-07",
        "total_hours": 40,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/timesheets/{existing_timesheet_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "period_start" in error_text, "Validation error should reference missing field"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_update_timesheet_returns_401
preconditions: no authentication token is provided
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet with a known ID exists
actions: PUT /api/timesheets/{timesheet_id} with a valid payload but no Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_unauthenticated_update_timesheet_returns_401(
    existing_timesheet_id,
):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "emp-001",
        "period_start": "2024-06-01",
        "period_end": "2024-06-07",
        "total_hours": 40,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/timesheets/{existing_timesheet_id}",
        json=payload,
        # No Authorization header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_update_timesheet_returns_403
preconditions: a user with role 'employee' (not hr-admin) is authenticated
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet with a known ID exists
actions: PUT /api/timesheets/{timesheet_id} with a valid payload and employee-role token
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_forbidden_role_update_timesheet_returns_403(
    employee_token,        # <fixture>: returns Bearer token for role=employee
    existing_timesheet_id,
):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "emp-001",
        "period_start": "2024-06-01",
        "period_end": "2024-06-07",
        "total_hours": 40,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/timesheets/{existing_timesheet_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_timesheet_boundary_zero_hours
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is available
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet with a known ID exists
actions: PUT /api/timesheets/{timesheet_id} with total_hours set to exactly 0 (minimum boundary)
expected: HTTP status code is 200 or 201 if 0 is a valid lower bound, OR 400 if 0 is below the minimum accepted value
Response body is consistent with the applied business rule for minimum hours
trace: R-WEB-018: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-018: boundary value for total_hours numeric field

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_timesheet_state_transition_draft_to_submitted
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is available
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet in 'draft' status exists with a known ID
actions: GET /api/timesheets/{timesheet_id} to confirm initial state is 'draft'
PUT /api/timesheets/{timesheet_id} with status='submitted' and all mandatory fields
GET /api/timesheets/{timesheet_id} to confirm final state is 'submitted'
Query audit log for state change record
expected: Audit record captures the state transition from draft to submitted
trace: R-WEB-018: audit record written on success
R-WEB-018: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_hr_admin_update_timesheet_state_transition_draft_to_submitted(
    hr_admin_token,
    draft_timesheet_id,   # <fixture>: seeds and returns id of a timesheet in 'draft' state
    audit_log_client,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    pre = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/timesheets/{draft_timesheet_id}", headers=headers)
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json().get("status") == "draft"
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "emp-002",
        "period_start": "2024-06-01",
        "period_end": "2024-06-07",
        "total_hours": 35,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    put_resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/timesheets/{draft_timesheet_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    post = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/timesheets/{draft_timesheet_id}", headers=headers)
    assert post.status_code == 200
    assert post.json().get("status") == "submitted"
    audit = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=draft_timesheet_id, action="update")
    assert any(r.get("from_status") == "draft" and r.get("to_status") == "submitted" for r in audit)
